title: Java Deque
description: Use ArrayDeque as both a stack and a double-ended queue.
learningGoals: Push and pop from one end of an ArrayDeque to use it as a stack.
Add and inspect both ends with offerLast, peekFirst, and peekLast.

completionChecks.prompt: What does stack.pop() return after pushing 1 then 2?
completionChecks.explanation: push and pop use the front of the deque as a stack, so the most recently pushed value comes out first.

completionChecks.prompt: What is a common ArrayDeque exam trap?
choices: It does not permit null elements
It only works as a FIFO queue
completionChecks.explanation: ArrayDeque supports stack and queue operations but rejects null elements, unlike some legacy APIs.

completionChecks.prompt: What prints after push(1), push(2), pop(), and offerLast(9)?
completionChecks.explanation: push and pop use the front of the deque, then offerLast appends 9 at the opposite end.
